objects.name: Banking Organization (TTX participant)
objects.description: Bank operating cloud-hosted digital onboarding and mortgage workflows.

objects.name: Cloud document storage for digital banking
objects.description: Cloud storage containing onboarding documents, ID images, and mortgage application artifacts.

objects.name: Expose banking documents through cloud storage misconfiguration
objects.description: Sensitive banking data becomes publicly accessible due to misconfigured permissions.

objects.name: Cloud storage exposure of customer application files
objects.description: Simulated data exposure caused by a cloud permissions error in customer-document storage.
Simulated observation that unauthenticated access to customer documents is possible from the internet.

objects.content: A security researcher informs the bank that customer mortgage and onboarding documents are reachable from a public URL path without authentication.
The bank must determine exposure duration, affected customer count, evidence of access, and whether immediate regulator and customer notification is required.

objects.name: Immediate cloud access lockdown and logging review
objects.description: Revoke public access, preserve logs, and validate retention for exposure window analysis.

objects.name: Cloud configuration guardrails for sensitive banking data
objects.description: Implement preventive policy checks, asset inventory controls, and approval gates for public exposure changes.

objects.abstract: Inject 3: external interest and operational pressure
objects.content: A journalist contacts corporate communications, stating they received a tip that a bank cloud repository exposed customer mortgage records. At the same time, business leadership asks whether digital onboarding can continue safely, and the fraud team requests a list of potentially affected customers for enhanced monitoring. Social media monitoring shows early speculation but no leaked files have appeared publicly.

objects.abstract: Inject 4: containment complete, governance questions remain
objects.content: The cloud team confirms public access has been removed. Preliminary review suggests the issue resulted from a permissions error introduced during a storage configuration change. There is still no conclusive evidence of mass download, but the organization cannot rule out third-party access during the exposure period. Leadership requests a formal incident summary, notification recommendation, and control-improvement plan.

objects.name: TTX Scenario 6 - Cloud storage misconfiguration and customer document exposure
objects.description: Scenario tests exposure scoping, notification timing, and cloud governance for sensitive customer data.
